zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Hulse於1415年出生在英國。她和John Kryton至少有1個兒子。她去世於1457年享年42歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret Hulse于1415年出生在英国。她和John Kryton至少有1个儿子。她去世于1457年享年42岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hulse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): habitational name from Hulse in Witton (Cheshire), from Middle English holes, plural of hole ‘hole, hollow, depression’ (Old English holh). See Hole .
North German (Hülse): topographic name for someone who lived where holly grew, Middle Low German huls, hüls.
American shortened and altered form of Flemish Holsaert (see Hulsart ). Compare Hulce , Huls , and Hults 1.

variants.name: Hulsart
meanings: Americanized form of Flemish Holsaert: unexplained. See also Hulse 3.

variants.name: Hole
meanings: English (southwestern): topographic name for someone who lived in or by a hole or hollow, from Middle English hol(e) (Old English hol, holh, dative hole), or a habitational name from some minor place so named.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads, so named from the dative singular or indefinite plural form of Old Norse hóll ‘round hill, mound’.

In some cases possibly also an American shortened form of Flemish Van Hole: habitational name from the common placename Hol(e), meaning ‘hollow, valley’; or a topographic name from the same term or from Middle Dutch hole, heule ‘(low) arched bridge, weir’.

variants.name: Hults
meanings: Altered form of   Hulse  , a surname of Flemish (and also of English and German) origin. Compare   Hultz   1.
In some cases also a variant of   Hultz   2, a surname of German origin.

variants.name: Hulce
meanings: Altered form of   Hulse  , a surname of Flemish (and also of English and German) origin.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Hulse was born in 1415, in England. She had at least 1 son with John Kryton. She died in 1457, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Hulse est née en 1415, à Angleterre. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec John Kryton. Elle est décédée en 1457, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Hulse wurde 1415 in England geboren. Sie hatte mit John Kryton mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1457 im Alter von 42 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Hulse è nata il nel 1415 nel England. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con John Kryton. Morì nel 1457, all'età di 42 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Hulseは1415年にイギリスで生まれた。彼女はJohn Krytonとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1457年に42歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Hulse(은)는 1415년 영국에서 태어났다. 그녀는 John Kryton(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1457년 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Hulse nasceu em 1415, em Inglaterra. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com John Kryton. Ela faleceu em 1457, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Hulse родилась в 1415 г. в местности Англия. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от John Kryton. Она умерла в 1457 г. в возрасте 42 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Hulse nació en 1415, en Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con John Kryton. Murió en 1457, a la edad de 42 años.
